data.title: AI TRENDS | Vitalik Buterin Explores Secure AI Personal Use Solutions
data.description:
Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin has detailed his exploration of localized, private, and secure AI personal use solutions. According to PANews, Buterin highlighted significant privacy and security concerns within the current AI landscape, including local open-source AI. He pointed out vulnerabilities such as OpenClaw agents modifying critical settings without human confirmation and malicious external inputs easily taking over user instances. Some skills even contain harmful instructions.

Buterin advocates for prioritizing local inference and sandbox isolation for all large language models (LLMs) and documents. He conducted tests using hardware like the NVIDIA 5090 laptop and AMD Ryzen AI Max Pro, employing the Qwen3.5:35B model through llama-server and utilizing the NixOS system.

data.title: Crypto Financing in Q1 2026 Surpasses $10 Million for 53 Projects, Prediction Market Leads with $1.67 Billion
data.description:
In the first quarter of 2026, 53 crypto projects secured financing exceeding $10 million, with the prediction market sector leading the way at $1.67 billion. According to PANews, this significant investment highlights the growing interest and confidence in the crypto industry.

However, the quarter also witnessed a major security breach as Drift Protocol suffered a hack resulting in losses exceeding $200 million. This incident has sparked a deep reflection on the security mechanisms within the decentralized finance (DeFi) sector, emphasizing the need for enhanced protective measures to safeguard against such vulnerabilities.

data.title: Aster Transitions to Privacy-Focused Layer 1 Application Chain
data.description:
Aster is undergoing a transformation from a decentralized derivatives trading protocol to a Layer 1 application chain centered on privacy transactions. According to PANews, with the launch of its mainnet and the introduction of staking and governance mechanisms, ASTER is evolving from a functional token to a core asset with both income and governance rights.

This article delves into Aster's transformation path, its token economic model, and market differences, focusing on how the buyback and staking mechanisms are reshaping ASTER's valuation logic. It also analyzes whether Aster can shift from being narrative-driven to fundamentally-driven in the future.

data.title: South Korea's People Power Party Proposes Scrapping Crypto Tax
data.description: South Korea's People Power Party has put forward a proposal to eliminate a 22% tax on cryptocurrency trading profits, which is set to be implemented on January 1, 2027. According to NS3.AI, the proposed tax would apply to annual crypto trading profits exceeding $1,665. This legislative move comes just weeks before the June local elections and follows previous delays in implementing the tax in 2020, 2022, and 2024.

data.title: Institutional Crypto Custody Evolves with Connected Infrastructure
data.description: Institutional crypto custody is undergoing a transformation as assets under professional management surpass $200 billion. According to NS3.AI, Komainu CEO Paul Frost-Smith highlighted a shift towards connected infrastructure in the industry. Meanwhile, Sam Boboev emphasized the necessity for institutions to align legal, compliance, and operational layers to mitigate potential coordination risks associated with faster settlement processes.

data.title: Tiger Research: Traditional Finance Apps May Pressure Crypto Exchanges in Asia
data.description: A study conducted by Tiger Research across nine countries suggests that banks, banking apps, and Bitcoin ETFs might exert pressure on cryptocurrency exchanges, particularly in Asia. According to NS3.AI, the report highlights that reduced trading activity in South Korea, coupled with the convenience offered by traditional finance applications, could expedite this transition.

data.title: U.S. Employment Trends Show Moderate Slowdown Amid Strike Resolutions and Weather Improvements
data.description: According to Jin10, the U.S. employment trends are experiencing a moderate slowdown, with recent strike resolutions and improved weather conditions expected to provide a short-term boost. However, these factors are seen as temporary disruptions rather than indicators of an economic acceleration. The Federal Reserve is likely to maintain its current stance, with a focus on whether unemployment rates and wages weaken simultaneously.

data.title: Elliptic Identifies North Korean Traits in Drift Protocol Attack
data.description:
Blockchain analysis firm Elliptic has identified characteristics of North Korean state-sponsored hackers in the $285 million attack on Drift Protocol. According to Foresight News, the incident involved premeditated, meticulously planned on-chain activities and organized cross-chain laundering processes, resembling previous cryptocurrency thefts linked to North Korea.

If confirmed, this would mark the 18th North Korean-related criminal activity tracked by Elliptic this year. So far, security incidents associated with North Korean hackers have amounted to over $300 million. In recent years, individuals linked to North Korea have stolen more than $6.5 billion in crypto assets.

data.title: U.S. prosecutors in Connecticut recover $600,000 in cryptocurrency assets obtained through a Ledger phishing scam.
data.description: The U.S. Attorney's Office for the Southern District of Connecticut announced the recovery and forfeiture of over $600,000 in cryptocurrency after a Ledger hardware wallet user fell victim to a phishing scam in September 2025. The user was tricked into performing a so-called security check by a letter impersonating "Ledger Security and Compliance," resulting in the theft of approximately $234,000 in crypto assets. The FBI and state police traced the funds, successfully seizing approximately $600,000 in USDT and filing a civil forfeiture lawsuit, alleging that the funds originated from wire fraud and money laundering. This is reportedly one of a series of phishing attacks targeting hardware wallet users, where scammers used physical mail containing company logos, holograms, and QR codes to increase the credibility of the fraud. Similar incidents have affected companies such as Ledger and Trezor, leading to data breaches and vulnerabilities in third-party services, triggering ongoing phishing attacks. (Decrypt)

data.title: Iran will impose tolls on ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz.
data.description: Iran's Deputy Foreign Minister announced that Iran will impose passage fees on ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz. (Jinshi)

data.title: Federal Reserve's Logan: Restoring Inflation to 2% is Crucial
data.description: Federal Reserve official Logan emphasized the importance of returning inflation to the 2% target. According to Jin10, Logan stated that achieving this goal is vital for economic stability and growth. The statement underscores the ongoing efforts by the Federal Reserve to manage inflationary pressures and maintain a balanced economic environment.

data.title: X Platform Implements Anti-Phishing Measures to Combat Crypto Scams
data.description: X Product Lead Nikita Bier announced that the platform is advancing a security mechanism to counter phishing incidents related to cryptocurrency. According to Foresight News, the new system will automatically lock accounts and initiate identity verification if a user posts crypto-related content for the first time in their account history. This measure is expected to eliminate 99% of scam attempts, particularly addressing the lack of action from Google in preventing phishing emails.

data.title: Entity Withdraws 6,328 ETH from Exchange
data.description: A single entity has withdrawn a total of 6,328 ETH from an exchange using two newly created linked wallets. According to ChainCatcher, this transaction was monitored by Onchain Lens.

data.title: Polymarket Predicts Shortened U.S. Homeland Security Shutdown
data.description:
The probability of a U.S. Department of Homeland Security shutdown lasting more than 70 days has significantly decreased to 8%, according to Odaily. This marks a 45% drop in the past 24 hours, with the event contract's trading volume exceeding $1.27 million. The contract stipulates that if the shutdown, starting from February 14, 2026, lasts at least the number of days listed in the market, it will settle as 'Yes'; otherwise, it will settle as 'No'. The end date of the shutdown will be determined by the date a funding bill to restart the department is signed by the President or otherwise enacted, with mere announcements of a restart not being considered.

U.S. President Donald Trump recently announced plans to sign an order to pay all employees of the Department of Homeland Security. He also expressed gratitude to House Speaker Johnson and Thune for their efforts this week and praised congressional Republicans for making progress on the Homeland Security issue.

data.title: Two newly created wallets withdrew 6,328 ETH from Binance, worth $12.98 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens, two newly created wallets withdrew 6,328 ETH from Binance, worth $12.98 million. These two wallets may belong to the same entity.

data.title: Both WTI and Brent crude oil prices fell sharply in the short term, with WTI crude oil trading at $108.93 per barrel.
data.description: According to Gate data, both WTI and Brent crude oil prices fell sharply in the short term, with WTI crude dropping nearly $5 to $108.93 per barrel. Reports indicate that Iran is drafting a passage agreement for the Strait of Hormuz with Oman.

data.title: Product Manager X: An automatic locking and verification mechanism will be implemented for accounts that post encrypted content for the first time.
data.description: Nikita Bier, the platform's product lead and Solana ecosystem advisor, stated that the X platform is implementing a countermeasure against cryptocurrency phishing scams: if an account posts cryptocurrency-related content for the first time in its history, the platform will automatically lock the account and require identity verification, which is expected to eliminate 99% of malicious motives.

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Surpasses 67,000 USDT with a Narrowed 1.97% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Apr 02, 2026, 14:45 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has crossed the 67,000 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 67,082.851563 USDT, with a narrowed narrowed 1.97% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Murad Mahmudov Predicts SPX6900's Market Cap Could Hit $1 Trillion Despite Portfolio Decline
data.description: Murad Mahmudov has expressed optimism that SPX6900 could achieve a market capitalization of $1 trillion, despite a significant drop in his memecoin portfolio. According to NS3.AI, Mahmudov's portfolio experienced a decline of approximately $60 million from its peak. Data from Arkham Intelligence reveals that wallets labeled 'Muststopmurad' contained around 29.964 million SPX, valued at approximately $7.79 million on Wednesday, representing about 96% of his tracked portfolio.

data.title: US bank and crypto industry executives will conduct a "small-scale" review of the stablecoin yield agreement text in order to resolve the dispute as quickly as possible.
data.description: Crypto journalist Eleanor Terrett reported on the X platform that a small group of banking and crypto industry executives will review the text of a new stablecoin yield agreement over the next two days, aiming to resolve the lobbying dispute over stablecoin yields in the previous CLARITY bill as soon as possible. It is understood that the text will be limited to viewing by specific parties and will not be made more widely publicized. Those who visited Capitol Hill last week were able to review the text on-site but were not allowed to bring copies. Coinbase Chief Legal Officer Paul Grewal previously stated in an interview with Fox Business that the debate over stablecoin yields in the upcoming CLARITY bill is "very close" to reaching an agreement.
